content.full_text:
State of California 
Memorandum 
To 
: Opinion Request meeting participants. 
Date 
: Apr. 14, 1977 
From 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
Mike Baker 
Subject: Item for discussion. 
The State Bar has asked whether an attorney has to disclose 
the names of his clients in the following two situations: 
(1) 
The attorney's law firm is located or doing business, 
etc., within his official jurisdiction, but his client 
is located outside the jurisdiction. 
(2) 
The attorney's law firm is located outside the juris-
diction and the client is located within the jurisdic-
tion. 
MB:JO

sections.question_synthetic: Whether attorneys must disclose client names when their law firms or clients are located within or outside their official jurisdictions.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an internal FPPC memorandum, not a formal advice letter response. No formal Q&A sections present. Document appears to be an agenda item for discussion rather than a completed opinion letter with conclusions. The question is presented for discussion purposes at a meeting.
